data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: The time it takes to make a decision increases with the number and complexity of choices.
data.technical_spec: Minimize complexity and present clear options to streamline decision-making.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The time to acquire a target is a function of the distance to and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Optimize clickable areas and their proximity to improve usability.

data.title: Law of Prägnanz
data.description: People will perceive and interpret ambiguous or complex images as the simplest form(s) possible.
data.technical_spec: Design interfaces using clear, clean, and easily recognizable patterns.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: The average person can only keep 7 (plus or minus 2) items in their working memory.
data.technical_spec: Segment information and avoid overwhelming users with too much data at once.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, so they prefer your site to work the same way.
data.technical_spec: Adhere to standard conventions to ensure users know how to navigate your product.
